query: Do we already have a finding model for cerebral microbleeds? If not, please add one.
I'd like to add a synonym for pleural effusion — radiologists sometimes write 'fluid in the pleural space'. Can you update that model?
Please create a new finding model for cavum septum pellucidum — anatomic variant, head CT context.
Does defs/ have anything for intracranial aneurysm? If yes, let me view it; if no, create it.
Let's draft a finding model for medial meniscus tear. It should have presence, change from prior, and a morphology attribute.
Add 'CMB' and 'microhemorrhage' as synonyms to the cerebral microbleeds model if that finding exists.
Check whether we have 'nephrolithiasis' as a finding model. I think it's there under a different name.
I want to create a model for pneumocephalus. It'll share a contributor and source with the rest of this session.
Can you fix the description on the existing subdural hematoma model — the current one is unclear. Also add 'SDH' as a synonym.
Does the library include a finding for mass effect? If it's there, what does it look like; if not, draft one.
Process this whole CSV of findings — lists/headct_findings.csv — and create stubs for anything not already in the library.
Review all the fracture finding models in defs/ and flag anything whose change-from-prior values look wrong.
Audit defs/*_hemorrhage.fm.json for naming and synonym issues.
What's the best way to write a description for a radiology finding?
Run the validator script on the defs/ directory.
Write me a Python function to parse a DICOM header.
I have a directory of markdown stubs describing 20 new findings. Please create models for all of them.
Generate a summary of all recent commits to the main branch.
Explain the difference between SNOMED and RadLex coding.
Walk through every finding model tagged 'brain' and tell me what needs improvement.
